summary: Privileged Windows service installed at postinstall executes remote commands from a vendor IM channel and auto-updates to @latest every 6 hours
details: On `npm install -g` with Windows admin privileges, scripts/post-install.js silently registers and starts a `claw-subagent-service` Windows Service via node-windows that runs service/daemon.js. The daemon authenticates to a RongCloud IM channel at `newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im` as user `guardserver` and processes message types COMMAND, CHAT_MESSAGE, CREATE/DELETE_OPENCODE_SESSION, DEVICE_CONTROL, and DEVICE_STATUS_REQUEST. handleDeviceControl in service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js accepts a remote `delete` command that calls `svcMgr.stop(); svcMgr.uninstall();` and unlinks `~/.claw-bridge/config.json`; COMMAND messages start/stop/restart the local OpenClaw service via shell scripts under command/win and command/linux. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js continuously transmits MAC address, hostname, service status, AI session contents, projects, tasks, and usage/cost events from `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` to the vendor's IM channel every 20-30 seconds. Independently, service/updater.js executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours without user confirmation from the privileged service context, so any future version published to the npm account is auto-installed and re-runs its postinstall as a Windows Service — the installer's exposure is not bounded by the version they originally chose. The combination of a privileged install-time-installed persistent service, a remote command channel that can stop/uninstall services and delete files on operator command, continuous host-identifier and AI-session exfiltration, and unattended privileged auto-update of @latest is a remote-management backdoor with a permanent supply-chain pivot.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
